title: MANGA NGE Okay which was first EVA anime or EVA manga
display_title: MANGA NGE Okay which was first EVA anime or EVA manga
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Okay, which was first: EVA anime or EVA manga?             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Feb 28, 2007 3:05 pm I've always heard all this crap that the anime of Eva was acutally first, (which is what I always believed), espiecally considering it's usually the other way around: first the manga of a series is released, THEN the anime) But the people on the Eva Mailing List* say otherwise; the manga in fact was first. :? Can somebody PLEASE clarify this for me? Thanks! *Any of you here that are members of that, just to clarify: NO I'm NOT trying to say the manga's inferior in my posts, all I'm saying is the manga versions of the characters suck. People seem to be missing my point with that, though. (As well somebody spouting out crap that Rei/Shinji AREN'T having a romance manga-wise when they CLEARLY are!) Ugh. I want to try to have an intelligent conversation on there, but maybe I should just give up? :roll: *sigh* Whatever....I really like this board, anyway. EMF and Shimai-duo (Sailormusic.net forums) are seriously like my online homes, so to speak. Even if we disagree with certain aspects of the series, who cares? Our mutual love of Eva keeps us together! I love you guys!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 28, 2007 3:33 pm The first stage of the manga was published before the anime had aired.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Wed Feb 28, 2007 3:46 pm My understanding is that the first part of the manga came out before the anime, but was quickly overtaken within months/weeks due to the snails pace at which manga's are typically released. It's not like the manga was out years before the anime or anything though; for all intents and purposes they were released at virtually the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 28, 2007 3:47 pm The manga is based upon the Anime. Even though the first chapter of the manga was released before the first episode aired, it was still based on the Ep. 1 script. The project was always conceived of as an anime. The anime came first in concept, planning design, ect. The early release of the 1st chapter of the manga is just a technicality, The anime came first.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 28, 2007 4:04 pm The concept for Evangelion was initially developped for animation. As such, production work on Evangelion began before Sadamoto ever put pencil to paper on the Evangelion manga. Many people make the mistake of saying that the manga is based on the anime, which is not technically true. The manga and anime are both developped from the same concept, but that concept however was intended for animation. I like to think of them as two separate but parallel productions. Evangelion first hit its audience as a manga, and later down the road as an anime which quickly overtook the story of the manga.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 02, 2007 4:20 am If the manga isn't based on the anime, then there are a LOT of "curious coincidences" happening. It's not so different that one could feasibly say he has built a truly unique vision up from the same starting foundation. (If you want to see some of that, compare the Escaflowne anime to the manga. YIKES!) He's doing way too much copy-pasta and general scavenging. For all intents and purposes, it's his version of the anime; to claim otherwise is to misdiagnose.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Mar 02, 2007 2:40 pm That's my problem with it, not only did he bastardize the characters, but he's not doing anything TRULY unique! Yeah, I'm hoping/assuming we'll get something different in Sadamoto's take of EoE, but that's not enough... *sigh* Just what are the members of EML smoking saying it's unique and or great? Espeically saying the manga was first! :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 02, 2007 4:35 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: That's my problem with it, not only did he bastardize the characters, but he's not doing anything TRULY unique!

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:42 am Reichu wrote: If the manga isn't based on the anime, then there are a LOT of "curious coincidences" happening. <snip> For all intents and purposes, it's his version of the anime; to claim otherwise is to misdiagnose.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 12:47 am ORAMI!? wrote: And you wonder why ML people dislike this place...

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:03 am if he wants to bring people back to the manga he needs to hurry up and finish it

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:05 am Fearthebait wrote: if he wants to bring people back to the manga he needs to hurry up and finish it

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:06 am Reichu wrote: Fearthebait wrote: if he wants to bring people back to the manga he needs to hurry up and finish it

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Mar 04, 2007 1:07 am Straight from the horse's mouth: Sadamoto wrote: Q: Concentrating on the story, where do you think the biggest difference is between the manga and the anime? A: Well I did write the script of the manga using the anime as a base . And at the moment, I think they're pretty much the same. I've made the story more compact. I think even if you rephrase a sentence into less words, you're still saying the same thing. But the manga does have a different approach. Maybe I should say it has a differnt coreography. The main point is that the anime has quite a few different people writing the continuity, for example when the assistant director, Mr. Tsurumaki wrote the continuity for a particular script, and when Masayuki wrote a continuity for the same script, it came out to be a completely different program. I think there's a difference there.

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Sun Mar 04, 2007 2:39 am Reichu wrote: ORAMI!? wrote: And you wonder why ML people dislike this place...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 3:13 am ORAMI!? wrote: Not from what I have seen. At least no more than any online NGE circle likes to think of itself as more knowledgable or better than the others, these forums included.

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Sun Mar 04, 2007 3:58 am Reichu wrote: Admin's Note : The attitude problem is unnecessary. Please try to curb it in future posts. Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 04, 2007 4:05 am ORAMI!? wrote: And so is quoting my original post after I had edited it to take out unnecessary attitude (there was no hostility intended). I guess being an admin has its privileges...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 04, 2007 10:15 am ORAMI!? wrote: Straight from the horse's mouth:* http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evangelion/2006-November/003855.html And I know you are aware of this since you appear to participate on the EML.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 04, 2007 11:22 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Evangelion Mailing List wrote: ....Gendo and Fuyutsuki were modeled after Commander Ed Staker and Col. Alec Freeman from the TV series "UFO".
